description:
Follow-up from fz-ul4.16.3. Today, an Item::MacroCall inside a defmodule body — e.g.

  defmodule Constants do
    make_const(:pi_ish, 314)
  end

— produces an Item::Fn with bare name (pi_ish), not qualified (Constants.pi_ish). This is because the pipeline runs flatten BEFORE expand_items: flatten sees the MacroCall, can't expand it (no interp loaded), and just hoists it to top-level.

## Approach

Two real fixes:

1. Reorder: parse -> expand_items -> flatten -> expand_with. expand_items runs on un-flattened AST. load_program needs to handle pre-flatten Item::Module (recurse, bind fns under bare names) so the macros can be invoked.
2. Or annotate Item::MacroCall with a parent_module path during parse / flatten, and have expand_items prefix produced fn names with that path before splicing.

Option 1 is cleaner once load_program can do pre-flatten loading.

## Tests

- defmacro inside a module produces fns that resolve under Mod.<name>
- Macro invocation outside a module (top-level) still produces bare-named fns
- Test runner (.16.4) tests this when each test fixture is its own module
title: Item-macros inside defmodule produce qualified fn names
